title: tool/closure_archive.hexa — mtime-based archival of aged PSC-loop closure JSONs
summary: 126 closure JSONs accumulated in /Users/ghost/core/anima/state/. Older ones (e.g. early Mk.I-floor cycles) are reference material that bloats active scan paths. This cycle ships a sweeper-safe .hexa archiver: scans omega_*.json by mtime, default DRY-RUN, --apply moves files older than --age-days N (default 7) into archive/YYYY-MM/ subdirs. Atomic via mv on same filesystem. Parallel-safe via 60s race-window mtime guard + omega_meta_*.json + omega_cleanup_*.json self-protection guard.

predecessors.omega_meta_1: closure_index.hexa — flat index over the 123-126 closures (indexer scans whole dir, so archiving improves scan ergonomics over time)
predecessors.omega_meta_2: closure_graph.hexa — DAG navigator; identifying ω-cleanup-1 as natural follow-up to keep graph scan footprint bounded

context.problem: 126 closures in one flat dir works today but doesn't scale. Manual triage would be a chore. Automated mtime-based archival keeps the active dir focused on recent cycles while preserving full history at archive/YYYY-MM/ structure.
design_principles_honoured: DEFAULT DRY-RUN — never destructive without explicit --apply
ATOMIC mv on same filesystem (anima/state and anima/state/archive share mountpoint)
PARALLEL-SAFE via 60s race window + self-protection — other agents may concurrently write new closures
SELF-PROTECTION — omega_meta_*.json and omega_cleanup_*.json never archive themselves (prevents next-day rerun from chewing on this cycle's own closure)
NEVER DELETE — only mv. Archive dir is preserved indefinitely.

files_created.design_choice: python3 sub-exec for stat + classification (hexa stage1 lacks ergonomic mtime arithmetic). hexa side does --apply gating, mv invocation, sentinel emission, output formatting. Python is classification-only.
files_created.shell_pattern: write py to /tmp/closure_archive_<pid>.py → exec python3 with shell redirection → read back stdout+stderr → cleanup tmp

validation.result: __CLOSURE_ARCHIVE__ DRY_RUN total=10 moved=5 kept=5 threshold_days=7; source dir untouched
__CLOSURE_ARCHIVE__ APPLIED total=10 moved=5 kept=5; source has only 5 omega_fresh_*.json + new archive/ subdir; archive/2026-03/ contains all 5 omega_old_*.json files
2 SKIP_SELF (meta+cleanup) + 1 WOULD_MOVE (bridge_99); DRY_RUN total=3 moved=1 kept=2

validation_caveats.remote_routing_interference: On this development host, /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/hexa wrapper sometimes routes commands through hexa_remote (hetzner). When the remote is up but the script's python3 sub-exec hangs through that path, the test commands hung indefinitely. Workaround: invoke /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp directly — bypasses the remote-routing wrapper. The tool itself is correct; the routing is an environment idiosyncrasy of this dev host.
validation_caveats.implication: production launchd / cron environments invoking via $HOME/.hx/bin/hexa may experience the same remote-routing if hetzner is reachable. Mitigation in plist would be HEXA_LOCAL=1 in EnvironmentVariables — same as bridge-20's plist.

scope_constraints_honoured: hexa-only-suffix (raw 9): single new .hexa, no .sh / .py / .rs files added (the python is INLINE-WRITTEN to /tmp at runtime, not a tracked sibling file)
raw 71 report-only: DRY-RUN default; no destructive actions without --apply
raw 77 append-only respect: archived files are MOVED, not deleted; archive dir grows monotonically
no parallel-session collision: only NEW tool/closure_archive.hexa added; no edit to any parallel-session-locked path
no commit in anima — closure JSON written as untracked
agent_handoff_note: ω-cleanup-1 was launched as a background agent that hit Anthropic's user-account daily limit (resets 2:40am Asia/Seoul) at 8 tool uses — but had already written the 379-LOC tool to disk before the limit. Main session validated + closed the cycle directly. closure JSON + commit are MAIN-SESSION work, not agent's.
